summary: Hermes model usage must be routed, capped, logged, context-limited, and cost-estimated. The model is ONE tool in Hermes orchestration — not the only brain.

pricing.unknown_pricing_behavior: Cost estimated as $0 or 'pricing not configured'. Hermes reports token count but not dollar amount.
pricing.source_of_truth: Provider billing is the source of truth. All estimates are approximate.

no_model.description: Use local/router answer only. Most answers should be here. Cost: $0.

cheap_model.description: Use cheap/local/fallback model only if enabled. Typical cost: <$0.001.

primary_model.description: Use OpenRouter/main model only for higher-value reasoning. Typical cost: ~$0.001.

blocked_or_gated.description: Never direct model execution. Cost: $0 (model not called).

cost_reduction_strategies: Use no_model for status questions (how many rows, is it running, what is the source)
Use cheap_model for short rewrites (rewrite this, simplify, briefly explain)
Reserve primary_model for strategy and deep reasoning only
Keep context packets small — Hermes packs only relevant excerpts
Avoid sending full reports to the model
Cap output length (1200 for primary, 500 for cheap)
Avoid recursive model loops — each question gets one model call max
Ask Hermes to summarize one report instead of the full audit

background_jobs.policy: Background jobs that need model must use cheap_model unless Ray approves. Stop after repeated failure. Log every model call with cost estimates.

safety_rules: No frontend API keys — all provider keys in Supabase Edge Function secrets only
No fake exact billing claims — cost is always estimated
No model calls for dangerous execution
Firewall scans message and context before any external model call
